chapter_context.summary: The rebellion of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram against Moses and Aaron, resulting in a dramatic divine judgment.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on Korah's Rebellion (16:1-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Numbers.
This section focuses on Judgment of the Rebels (16:20-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Numbers.
This section focuses on Atonement for the Congregation (16:36-50), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Numbers.

key_themes.title: God's Faithfulness
key_themes.description: God sustaining His people despite their repeated rebellions.

key_themes.title: The Census
key_themes.description: The organization and numbering of the new generation.

devotional.question: How can we learn from Israel's mistakes and choose trust over complaining?
In what ways has God 'cloud and fire' guided your path recently?

cultural_historical.title: The Wilderness Journey
cultural_historical.text: The journey from Sinai to Moab was meant to be brief but became a 40-year period of purification for the nation.
